findings.finding: 152 references to 'demanda' across 6+ files. Heaviest in gdp-estoque-intel.js (~40 refs), gdp-init.js (~10 refs), gdp-pedidos.js (~15 refs), gdp-core.js (~10 refs), app-state.js (~7 refs). _selectedDemandaIds is a Set for bulk ops. Removal requires commenting/removing in all files but does NOT affect portal escolar (demandas are internal only).
findings.risk: MEDIUM — Many references but all internal. No external API depends on demanda.
findings.recommendation: Remove in Wave 3. Comment out (not delete) initially. Mark with // REMOVED: demanda (Regra 5).

findings.finding: 11 references across app-state.js, app.js, gdp-core.js, gdp-estoque-intel.js. Key: gdp.conversoes.v1 localStorage key. Used in app.js line 2489 for fator calculation. Replacement: produto_critico boolean per product (FR-013).
findings.risk: LOW — Minimal references, clear replacement strategy.
findings.recommendation: Remove in Wave 4 when produto_critico is added.

findings.topic: SKU strategy in contratos — current state
findings.finding: 111+ SKU references. Priority hierarchy already exists: skuVinculado (manual) > auto-match > empty (AC-9 rule in gdp-core.js line 638-639). skuVinculado is ALREADY the preferred approach. FR-005 aligns with existing AC-9 rule — just make it mandatory and remove auto-match.
findings.risk: LOW — Already partially implemented via AC-9 rule.
findings.recommendation: Wave 2: Make skuVinculado the ONLY path. Rename to produto_vinculado_id. Remove auto-match fallback.

findings.finding: Portal integration lives in app-sgd-integration.js (varrerSgd function). Data flows: SGD → orcamentos → pré-orçamento → aprovação → envio. Pedidos from portal are created via sync flow, not direct gdp-pedidos.js calls. Functions: buildSgdPayload(), BrowserSgdClient.listBudgets(). NONE of these touch demanda, conversão, or match automático directly.
findings.risk: LOW — Portal integration is isolated from GDP simplification changes.
findings.recommendation: Leave app-sgd-integration.js, sgd-proxy.js, sync-pedidos.js, sync-entregas.js completely untouched.

findings.finding: 17 references to 'comprometido'. Logic: tracked as movement type in gdp-estoque-intel.js. Formula: Disponivel = Fisico - Comprometido. No 'transito' references found (never implemented). Removal simplifies to: estoque = {produto_id, quantidade_atual}.
findings.risk: MEDIUM — Need to recalculate existing estoque records. Migration should set quantidade_atual = fisico - comprometido for final snapshot.
findings.recommendation: Wave 4: Simplify estoque model. Migration: snapshot current disponivel as quantidade_atual.

findings.finding: Defined in gdp-banco-produtos.js lines 335-357. Maps bancoProdutos internal schema to bancoPrecos interface (Story 8.3). Currently defined but not actively called in dashboard code. Available for future use.
findings.risk: NONE — Facade exists and works. FR-003 removes the UI tab, not the data.
findings.recommendation: FR-003: Remove Banco de Preços TAB and CONFIG. Keep getCentralComoBancoPrecos() facade for API compatibility.

findings.finding: Latest migration: 013_preco_historico_aggregation.sql. Next migration will be 014. Schema changes needed: (1) Add produto_vinculado_id to contratos.itens, (2) Add produto_critico to produtos, (3) Simplify estoque table. All additive (ALTER TABLE) — no destructive changes.
findings.risk: LOW — All additive migrations. No data loss.
findings.recommendation: Single migration 014_gdp_simplification.sql with all schema changes.

findings.finding: Currently 10 functions (limit 12). GDP simplification does NOT require new serverless functions. All changes are frontend JS + Supabase schema. Safe.

radar-matcher.js (230 lines) handles Radar/Intel Preços matching (SGD → banco de precos). This is SEPARATE from GDP contrato matching. FR-001 changes apply ONLY to radar-matcher.js flow (add human approval gate). FR-005 changes apply to GDP contrato flow (eliminate auto-match, use produto_vinculado_id). Two separate systems — can be changed independently.
findings.risk: LOW — Clear separation between Radar and GDP matching.
findings.recommendation: Wave 1: Fix radar-matcher.js (FR-001). Wave 2: Fix GDP contrato matching (FR-005). Independent.

findings.finding: Pré-orçamento table (index.html line 282-294) has columns: Item, Marca, Qtd, Custo Unit., Margem%, Preço Unit., Total. NO unidade column. However, SGD orcamento items DO contain 'unidade' field (from school budget). Data is available in the orcamento object but not displayed.
findings.risk: NONE — Data exists, just needs to be shown.
findings.recommendation: Wave 1: Add <th>Unidade</th> column and render with color-coded badges.

technology_validation.supabase_alter_table: VERIFIED — Supabase supports ALTER TABLE via migrations. Used in migrations 006-013.
technology_validation.vanilla_js_dom_manipulation: VERIFIED — Codebase uses vanilla JS throughout. No framework dependency.
technology_validation.barcode_api: VERIFIED — gdp-estoque-intel-mobile.html already uses barcode scanning. FR-012 extends existing pattern.
technology_validation.localStorage_sync: VERIFIED — gdp-core.js has cloud sync infrastructure. Pattern reusable.

dependencies_map.FR-004 → FR-005 → FR-006: Chain — contrato/produto separation enables SKU cleanup
dependencies_map.FR-019 → FR-004: Depends on contrato/produto separation being in place
dependencies_map.FR-007 → FR-008: Saldo formula needs demanda removed first
dependencies_map.FR-009 → FR-010: Pedido as execution needs operations centralized
dependencies_map.FR-015 → FR-016 → FR-017: Menu + reports depend on module simplification

conclusion: All 19 FRs are technically feasible with the existing codebase. No new libraries, frameworks, or infrastructure needed. Wave-based approach validated — each wave is independently deployable. Highest risk is Wave 3 (pedido centralization + demanda removal) due to 152 demanda references. Portal escolar integration is confirmed safe.
